name: Formula One 2020
attribution: Historical data derived from f1db (github.com/f1db/f1db, CC BY 4.0)
AI driver identities AND AI ratings from the community NAMeS F-Ultimate.xml custom-AI set, matching the 2020 F-Ultimate skinpack
notes: Class: F-Ultimate (F-Ultimate.xml), which maps to the single vehicle model formula_ultimate_2019; every team is bound to that chassis.

AI ratings (race_skill, qualifying_skill and all supporting stats) are EXTRACTED verbatim from the installed NAMeS F-Ultimate.xml, not authored. The XML carries per-track (tracks="...") rating variants of each car; only the base per-car entry is carried here (variants deduped to one entry per driver).

Coverage: all 20 primary 2020 seats plus the four substitute drives the NAMeS set ships liveries for - Hulkenberg (Racing Point, rounds 4-5 & 11), Fittipaldi (Haas, rounds 16-17), Russell (Mercedes, Sakhir round 16) and Aitken (Williams, Sakhir round 16). Russell has two entries: his season Williams #63 seat (rounds 1-15,17) and his one-off Mercedes #63 stand-in (round 16).

Points system copied verbatim from the 2020 entry of the oracle catalog (data/rules/f1-points-systems.json): 25-18-15-... top ten, +1 fastest-lap point (classified top ten, counts for constructors), and the Racing Point -15 constructors brake-duct penalty.

Placeholder rounds (format v1): venues AMS2 does not ship (Mugello, Sochi, Portimao, Istanbul, Bahrain GP & Outer, Yas Marina) are reproduced on the nearest same-era AMS2 GP track with laps recomputed to preserve the historical race DISTANCE (realVenue set, isPlaceholder=true).

grid.size on every round is that round's covered f1db starters (20 cars each race); setupGuide opponents = grid.size - 1. Every mapped track's Max AI participants (>=29) comfortably exceeds the 20-car field.
